---
title: "AI Workflow Seminar"
subtitle: "Using Claude Code for Research"
author: "Coady Wing"
date: "2026-03-04"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, themes/clean.scss]
    scrollable: true
    slide-number: true
    embed-resources: true
    code-overflow: wrap
editor:
  markdown:
    wrap: 72
---

## Seminar Repository and Claude Code Quickstart

Repository for Today: [github.com/coadywing/ai_workflow_seminar](https://github.com/coadywing/ai_workflow_seminar)


Claude Code Quickstart: [code.claude.com/docs/en/quickstart](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/quickstart)


## Claude Code

Claude Code is a **command-line agent** that lives in your
terminal.

- It reads and writes files on your computer
- It runs shell commands
- It understands your project structure
- It follows instructions you give it in `CLAUDE.md`

## Prerequisites

It is not free but it is very cheap and well worth paying for.

- **Claude Pro** ($20/mo)
- **Claude Max** ($100/mo)
- **API console** at
  [console.anthropic.com](https://console.anthropic.com) — use
  an API key


## Conversations and Context

You talk to Claude in natural language. Key difference from a
chatbot: Claude Code can take actions on your computer.

- Write clear instructions: context, tasks, outcomes
- Use plan mode for complex tasks; redirect if you don't like
  the plan
- Iterate on the work product — turnaround is fast

The **context window** is Claude's working memory (~200,000
tokens). Keep sessions focused; use `/clear` when switching
tasks.

## `CLAUDE.md`: Project Memory

`CLAUDE.md` is the first file Claude reads when it starts. It
tells Claude what the project is about, how it's structured,
and what conventions to follow.

When you start a new project, describe it to Claude and ask it
to write a `CLAUDE.md`. Update this file as the project
evolves.

## The `.claude` folder

Hidden folder where you put configuration files.

- On a mac, view hidden directories with `Cmd+Shift+.` in
  Finder
- On windows, view hidden directories with `Ctrl+H` in File
  Explorer

## Rules: Automatic Conventions

Rules live in `.claude/rules/` and are loaded automatically.

Example from `r-code-conventions.md`:

- Use `data.table` (not tidyverse)
- Use `here()` for all paths
- Use `fixest` for regressions

Rules are natural language in markdown files. Scoping
conditions control when they are loaded.

## Skills: Slash Commands

Skills are pre-built prompts you invoke with `/`:

```
/proofread manuscript/3_results.tex
/review-r code/03_estimation.r
/compile-paper
```

Skills live in `.claude/skills/`. They encode complex
multi-step workflows into a single command.

## Agents

Main Claude can spawn agents to perform specialized tasks.

Each agent has its own context window. The agent performs its
task and returns a structured report to the main Claude.

## GitHub & Version Control

1. **Clean directory structure** — Git repos encourage the kind
   of organized project layout that Claude works best with

2. **Safety net** — if Claude makes big changes that don't work
   out, you can always roll back to the last commit

3. **Claude makes Git easier** — Claude can handle commits,
   diffs, and even pull requests for you
